content_preview.title: JWST Shows Neptune's Moon Nereid Is an Original Survivor, Not a Kuiper Belt Capture — Rewriting the Planet's Violent History
content_preview.summary: A Science Advances study using Webb data finds Nereid's icy composition rules out a Kuiper Belt origin, making it the sole intact remnant of Neptune's primordial moon system.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Neptune's moon Nereid — long assumed to be a wayward object captured from the outer solar system — is instead a native remnant of the planet's original satellite system that survived one of the most violent events in the solar system's history. A study published May 20 in *Science Advances* by a team at the California Institute of Technology used NASA's James Webb Space Telescope to analyze Nereid's surface composition for the first time and found it bears no resemblance to Kuiper B...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured, clearly written News article at 942 words. Appropriate depth for the category (400-1200 word range). Organizes the story logically: overview, Webb findings, the Triton scenario, observational history, and open questions. No sensationalism or editorializing.
editor_notes.source_verification: source-0 (phys.org): HTTP 403 bot-blocked, no snapshot available. All claims attributed to phys.org ('composition was inconsistent with Kuiper Belt objects — it had too much ice'; 'strongly rule out'; Belyakov quote 'What we know about Nereid is very limited. For its size, Nereid is extremely understudied') were independently confirmed in source-2 (news4jax.com, AP wire), which carries the same AP wire story content. source-1 (space.com, source-1.html.gz): Fully verified. All Space.com-attributed quotes and claims are verbatim: 'Nereid is a huge outlier', Kuiper 1949 discovery-paper quote, JWST/funding quote, and the Triton simulation quote. source-2 (news4jax.com, source-2.html.gz): Not on allowlist but carries AP wire content by Marcia Dunn (Associated Press). Confirms phys.org-attributed claims verbatim. source-3 (localnews8.com, source-3.html.gz): Not on allowlist but carries CNN Newsource content by Jacopo Prisco. Confirms Triton capture timeline, Belyakov 25% simulation statistic, CO2 signature, and 'disrupted rubble piles' characterization. MISQUOTE FOUND: Article states 'Belyakov concluded that "Nereid is the only intact survivor of this process."' The CNN source actually quotes Belyakov as saying '"I think Nereid is the only intact survivor of this process,"' — the article drops the hedging qualifier 'I think', turning a tentative first-person assessment into a definitive conclusion. This is a paraphrase inside quote marks.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All facts confirmed by available sources. Orbital distances (less than 1 million miles / 6 million miles), moon diameter (~220 miles / 350 km), Kuiper Belt origin skepticism, Triton retrograde orbit, 25% simulation survival rate, Webb 10-minute observation duration, DOI 10.1126/sciadv.aeb1429, and author names are all supported. The claim that Saturn has 292 moons (vs Neptune's 16) is present in the AP source. One misquote: 'Belyakov concluded that...' should include the 'I think' qualifier per the CNN source.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism. The word 'survivor' in the headline is a scientific framing used by the researchers themselves. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Nereid or Neptune moon coverage found in the article archive. Unique topic.
concerns: One direct quote strips the 'I think' hedge: article says Belyakov 'concluded' that Nereid is the only intact survivor, but the CNN source quotes him as saying 'I think Nereid is the only intact survivor of this process' — a probabilistic statement, not a conclusion. This is recoverable as a corrections note.

Two sources (news4jax.com, localnews8.com) are not on the source allowlist. Both are local TV station websites carrying wire content from AP and CNN respectively. The substantive journalism originates from credible wire services.
recommendations: Add news4jax.com, localnews8.com to source allowlist, or use the canonical AP/CNN URLs directly in future submissions.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid, well-sourced News article on a genuine scientific finding. One recoverable misquote (dropped 'I think' qualifier) warrants an APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS verdict. The article's substance is accurate and the story is well-told.
